name: Apple Brown Betty with Rum Whipped Cream
recipeIngredient: 1 stick (1/2 cup) Unsalted butter, chilled and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
4 slices of bread with crusts removed
3 Granny Smith apples, peeled, cored, and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions: To make the apple brown betty: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Butter a 9-inch-square baking dish. In a bowl, sift together the flour, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg, and sugar. Cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Cut the bread into 1-inch pieces and soak the raisins in boiling water for about 5 minutes, then drain them. Combine the bread, raisins, and sliced apples in a bowl. Sprinkle 3/4 cup of the flour mixture over the bottom of the prepared baking dish, add the apple mixture, and top with the remaining flour mixture. Bake uncovered in the preheated oven until the top is crisp and golden and the apples are tender, about 35 to 45 minutes. To make the rum whipped cream: In a chilled medium bowl, beat the cream and sugar until stiff. Beat in the rum. Serve the betty warm with the rum whipped cream. Serves 6.
